This amphibian appears to be a newt, likely a species from the Salamandridae family.
It shares characteristics with the Eastern Newt ( Notophthalmus viridescens ), specifically its brownish coloration and semi-aquatic nature.
Newts often have distinctive lifecycles, and this individual might be in a terrestrial stage, commonly known as an eft, or a juvenile aquatic stage.
While they have toxins for defense, they are gentle creatures.
